body.title: [sprint] qwen3-coder:30b vs devstral-24b — architect latency + does-it-plan probe
body.description:
**Problem:** Iteration velocity is the binding constraint on the whole builder program (devstral-small-2:24b runs ~4min/turn on Hex, so one live data point costs 30min-5h and one diagnosis cycle costs a day). qwen3-coder:30b (models_registry.py:296) is a 30B-A3B MoE with ~3B active params — likely materially FASTER than dense-24B on the same 32GB box, and stronger at coding. PRIOR ART (prior-art gate): T-aider-rack-device was cancelled after its root cause turned out to be tier-routing to qwen2.5:7b instead of a 32B model — with proper-tier routing, edits worked cleanly. So 'the model is below floor / wrong tier' is a REAL, untested confound for our loop, and this probe tests it for the price of two runs.

**Proposed shape:** Run the existing architect path (ArchitectEditorFlow / AgenticLoop plan_mode) on ONE real multi-file ticket, once with devstral-small-2:24b@Hex and once with qwen3-coder:30b@Hex. cwd = throwaway clone with RELATIVE paths only (124553ee contamination lesson), num_ctx=32768, flat-rate 600s wall. Pre-register the decision rule via prereg.py BEFORE running. Record per arm: wall-clock, ms/turn, turns-to-plan, and whether a substantive plan was produced (_is_substantive_plan). io_corpus already captures every byte; write a short results note.

**Affected files:** new probe runner under devlab/claudecode/ (reads the existing loop; no loop changes). LOW inertia.
**Design rules:** Inference-Proxy-only (route through the inference device, no direct OR/Anthropic calls); every-flag-stamps-io_corpus (aci_mode/plan_mode/role must appear on the corpus records for post-hoc classification).
**Scope boundary:** MEASURE only — no loop mutations. Picking the architect model default is a follow-up decision informed by this. Do NOT wire per-role tiering here.
**Test plan:** Observation-shaped. Red-form: assert the runner records both arms' (latency, ms/turn, plan-bool) AND a prereg record exists with an earlier timestamp than the results. If no honest red form, close shipped-unproven naming the lever = observation-shaped probe (per proof_emitter-cant-prove note).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Completion criteria:**
- prereg.py record written BEFORE either run (earliest-timestamp firewall holds).
- Both arms (devstral-24b, qwen3-coder:30b) ran on the SAME real multi-file ticket + throwaway clone; live repo untouched (0 live-repo path refs in io_corpus).
- Results note records per arm: wall-clock, ms/turn, turns-to-plan, plan-substantive bool.
- A comparison line states whether qwen is faster AND whether it produced a substantive plan — enough to decide the architect model default in a follow-up.
body.intention: I intend that the builder's model lever is finally PRICED — so we stop attributing the 0-edits wall to loop shape while an untested tier confound (T-aider-rack-device: 7B-not-32B) sits underneath. Serves the DS.0-leveling north star and iteration-velocity-as-binding-constraint.
